title: Pentacalia badilloi
intro: Pentacalia badilloi is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family, Asteraceae, native to the Andes mountains in South America. Traditionally, Pentacalia badilloi has been used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Pentacalia badilloi include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Pentacalia badilloi is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and it is also used in the production of essential oils and extracts, which are valued for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities.
